name: zephyr-python-api

metadata.name: zephyr-python-api
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Zephyr-python-api


![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/zephyr-python-api)
![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/zephyr-python-api)
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/zephyr-python-api)
### Project description
This is a set of wrappers for Zephyr Scale (TM4J) REST API. 
This means you can interact with Zephyr Scale without GUI, access it with python code and create 
automation scripts for your every day interactions.

For more detailed information please see [the project's documentation](https://zephyr-python-api.readthedocs.io/en/main/index.html).

To be done:
* More usage examples
* Tests, tests and tests for gods of testing
* Convenient docs
* Implementing higher level wrappers representing Test Case, Test Cycle, etc.

### Installation

```
pip install zephyr-python-api
```

### Example usage

Zephyr Cloud auth:
```python
from zephyr import ZephyrScale

zscale = ZephyrScale(token="<your_token>")
```

Zephyr Server (TM4J) auth:
```python
from zephyr import ZephyrScale

# Auth can be made with Jira token
auth = {"token": "<your_jira_token>"}

# or with login and password (suggest using get_pass)
auth = {"username": "<your_login>", "password": "<your_password>"}

# or even session cookie dict
auth = {"cookies": "<session_cookie_dict>"}

zscale = ZephyrScale.server_api(base_url="<your_base_url>", **auth)
```

Then it is possible to interact with api wrappers:
```python
zapi = zscale.api

# Get all test cases
all_test_cases = zapi.test_cases.get_test_cases()

# Get a single test case by its id
test_case = zapi.test_cases.get_test_case("<test_case_id>")

# Create a test case
creation_result = zapi.test_cases.create_test_case("<project_key>", "test_case_name")
```

### License

This library is licensed under the Apache 2.0 License.

### Links

[Zephyr Scale Cloud API docs](https://support.smartbear.com/zephyr-scale-cloud/api-docs/)

[Zephyr Scale Server API docs](https://support.smartbear.com/zephyr-scale-server/api-docs/v1/)

modules.name: zephyr.scale.scale
documentation: A module with the Zephyr Scale base object.

classes.name: zephyr.scale.scale.ZephyrScale

methods.name: zephyr.scale.scale.ZephyrScale.server_api
documentation:
Alternative constructor for Zephyr Scale Server client.

```
param base_url

base API url to connect with
```

Zephyr Scale base object to interact with raw Zephyr APIs or other Zephyr entities. You should
define the API version of Zephyr Scale instance you are going to work with. Server is v1 and
Cloud is v2.

NOTE: Cloud API accepts only token auth, whereas Server API works with Jira auth methods.

```
param base_url

base API url to connect with

param api_version

'v2' for Cloud and 'v1' for Server

raises ValueError

if api_version is not 'v1' or 'v2'
```

Zephyr Scale Server Api wrapper class. It contains API endpoint wrappers for the Zephyr Scale
Server/Datacenter. The wrappers are grouped by the entity they are related to.
These wrapper groups are represented by the properties of the class.

For more details on the API endpoints see docs:
<https://support.smartbear.com/zephyr-scale-server/api-docs/v1/>

```
param session

ZephyrSession object with auth credentials
```

classes.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.FolderEndpoints

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.FolderEndpoints.create_folder
documentation:
Creates a new folder for test cases, test plans or test runs.

In order to create a new folder you must POST a json with 3 fields: projectKey,
name and type. The field type can be filled with TEST_CASE, TEST_PLAN or TEST_RUN.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.FolderEndpoints.update_folder
documentation:
Updates a folder for test cases, test plans or test runs.

You can only update the name or the custom field value of a folder, in order to do that
you must PUT a json with 2 fields: name and customFields. The field name is a String and
forward and backslashes are not allowed. The field customFields is an object with
the key being the custom field name.
Api wrapper for "Folder" endpoints

Retrieve the Environments matching the given projectKey.

The project must exist.
The project must have Zephyr Scale enabled.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.EnvironmentEndpoints.create_environment

Creates a new Environment.

The project must exist
The project must have Zephyr Scale enabled
The name must be unique
Api wrapper for "Environment" endpoints

classes.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.AutomationEndpoints

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.AutomationEndpoints.create_cycle
documentation:
Creates a new Test Cycle based on provided automated test results.

This endpoint receives a zip file containing one or more Zephyr Scale Test Results File
Format to create the Test Cycle. See Zephyr Scale JUnit Integration
(https://bitbucket.org/smartbeartm4j/tm4j-junit-integration) to learn how
to generate this file. Optionally, you can send a testCycle part in your form data
to customize the created Test Cycle.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.AutomationEndpoints.create_cycle_cucumber
documentation:
Creates a new Test Cycle based on provided automated test results.

This endpoint receives a zip file containing one or more Cucumber Json Output file
(https://relishapp.com/cucumber/cucumber/docs/formatters/json-output-formatter).
Optionally, you can send a testCycle part in your form data to customize
the created Test Cycle.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.AutomationEndpoints.get_testcases_cucumber
documentation:
Retrieve a zip file containing Cucumber Feature Files
that matches the tql passed as parameter.
Api wrapper for "Automation" endpoints

classes.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.ProjectEndpoints

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.ProjectEndpoints.create_zephyr_project
documentation:
Create a Zephyr Scale project for an existing Jira project.

If the Zephyr Scale project exists, enable/disable it.
Api wrapper for "Project" endpoints

classes.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.CustomFieldEndpoints

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.CustomFieldEndpoints.create_custom_field
documentation:
Creates a new custom field for test cases, test plans, test runs, test result or folder.
The custom fied name must be unique by project and category.

Custom fields must have one of these categories:
TEST_PLAN, TEST_RUN, TEST_STEP, TEST_EXECUTION, TEST_CASE or FOLDER.

Custom fields must have of these types: SINGLE_LINE_TEXT, MULTI_LINE_TEXT, NUMBER, DATE,
SINGLE_CHOICE_SELECT_LIST, CHECKBOX, DECIMAL, MULTI_CHOICE_SELECT_LIST or USER_LIST.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.CustomFieldEndpoints.create_custom_field_opt
documentation:
Creates a new custom field option for SINGLE_CHOICE_SELECT_LIST or
MULTI_CHOICE_SELECT_LIST custom field.
Api wrapper for "Custom Field" endpoints

classes.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.DeleteExecutionEndpoints

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.DeleteExecutionEndpoints.delete_execution
documentation:
Starts the deletion process of Test Executions (also known as Test Results).

This process only removes executions older than 3 months and it will keep
the last test executions. Only Jira Admin users can execute this process.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.server.endpoints.endpoints.DeleteExecutionEndpoints.get_status
documentation:
Gets the status of the test execution deletion process.

The statuses can be: IN_PROGRESS, FINISHED or FAILED.
Api wrapper for "Delete Execution" endpoints

These wrapper groups are represented by the properties of the class.

For more details on the API endpoints see docs:
<https://support.smartbear.com/zephyr-scale-cloud/api-docs/>

```

ZephyrSession object with auth credentials
```

classes.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_test_cases
documentation:
Retrieves all test cases. Query parameters can be used to filter the results.

Keyword arguments:
\:keyword projectKey: Jira project key filter
\:keyword folderId: Folder ID filter
\:keyword maxResults: A hint as to the maximum number of results to return in each call
\:keyword startAt: Zero-indexed starting position. Should be a multiple of maxResults
\:return: dict with response body

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.create_test_case
documentation:
Creates a test case. Fields priorityName and statusName will be set to
default values if not informed. Default values are usually “Normal”
for priorityName and “Draft” for statusName. All required test case custom
fields should be present in the request.

```
param project_key

Jira project key

param name

test case name
```

Keyword arguments:
\:keyword objective: A description of the objective
\:keyword precondition: Any conditions that need to be met
\:keyword estimatedTime: Estimated duration in milliseconds
\:keyword componentId: ID of a component from Jira
\:keyword priorityName: The priority name
\:keyword statusName: The status name
\:keyword folderId: ID of a folder to place the entity within
\:keyword ownerId: Atlassian Account ID of the Jira user
\:keyword labels: Array of labels associated to this entity
\:return: dict with response body

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_test_case
documentation:
Returns a test case for the given key

```
param test_case_key

The key of the test case

return

dict with response body
```

```
param test_case_key

integer id of the test

param name

test case name

param project_id

project id

param priority_id

priority id

param status_id

status id

return

```

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_links
documentation: Returns links for a test case with specified key

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.create_issue_links
documentation: Creates a link between a test case and a Jira issue

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.create_web_links
documentation: Creates a link between a test case and a generic URL

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_versions
documentation:
Returns all test case versions for a test case with specified key.
Response is ordered by most recent first

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_version
documentation: Retrieves a specific version of a test case

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_test_script
documentation: Returns the test script for the given test case

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.create_test_script
documentation:
Creates or updates the test script for a test case. If the test case currently
has a sequence of test steps assigned to it, these will be implicitly removed.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.get_test_steps
documentation:
Returns the test steps for the given test case. Provides a paged response,
with 100 items per page.

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestCaseEndpoints.post_test_steps
documentation:
Assigns a series of test steps to a test case, appending them to any existing
sequence of test steps. A maximum of 100 steps can be posted per request. Consumers
should not attempt to parallelize this operation, as the order of the steps is defined
by the input order. If this endpoint is called on a test case that already has
a plain text or BDD test script, that test script will implicitly be removed.
All required step custom fields should be present in the request.

Api wrapper for "Test Case" endpoints

Details: https://support.smartbear.com/zephyr-scale-cloud/api-docs/#tag/Test-Cases

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestPlanEndpoints.get_test_plans
documentation:
Retrieves all test plans. Query parameters can be used to filter the results.

Keyword arguments:
\:keyword projectKey: Jira project key filter
\:keyword maxResults: A hint as to the maximum number of results to return in each call
\:keyword startAt: Zero-indexed starting position. Should be a multiple of maxResults
\:return: dict with response body

methods.name: zephyr.scale.cloud.endpoints.endpoints.TestPlanEndpoints.create_test_plan
documentation:
Creates a test plan. All required test plan custom fields

: should be present in the request.

```

Jira project key

test plan name

return

dict with response body
```
